legend.decision: adopt = fits o7d's state-ownership + security model largely as-is; adapt = reuse with defined changes; reference = learn from, do not import; reject = incompatible / anti-pattern for 007
legend.upstream_sync_risk: risk that tracking Consilium's evolution breaks the reuse
legend.effort: S/M/L/XL implementation effort for the 007 side

components.rationale: Clean per-CLI trait (build_command + pure parse_line/parse_final + classify_failure) is a good porting seam. But permission is a two-bool (advisory/write) mapped to raw CLI flags, and parsing is fail-open (unknown -> empty Vec).
required_changes: Replace two-bool permission with a first-class PermissionMode that supports live switching
Make unknown-event handling observable (count/log), not silent
Carry failure kind + rate-limit/reset on the event, not post-hoc string match
security_risks: fail-open parse can mask malformed/hostile CLI output
compatibility_risks: assumes one-shot CLI, not a long-lived SDK worker
required_acceptance_tests: unknown event is surfaced not dropped
permission mode change reflected in the next spawned argv

components.target_007_component: claude -p adapter (and reference for the Claude SDK worker)
components.rationale: Solid stream-json parsing and cache-token summing, with real recorded fixtures. But it DROPS the dedicated rate_limit_event line, never compares requested-vs-observed model, has no resume/multi-turn, and emits ToolCall at request-time.
required_changes: Parse and forward rate_limit_event
Emit a model-info event and compare to requested (normalizing the [1m]/context-window suffix — see Stage E probe)
Split tool start/complete; add tool_call_id
Wire resume/multi-turn for a long-lived worker
security_risks: dropped rate-limit signal blinds a BudgetPolicy
compatibility_risks: for the SDK worker, one-shot -p model must be replaced by a persistent session
required_acceptance_tests: rate_limit_event surfaces as a typed 007 event
requested/observed model mismatch produces a model-drift event

components.rationale: Correct usage accounting (no double-count of cached subset), good fixtures. But SessionStarted.model is hard-coded None, so exact-model verification is impossible for Codex; ToolCall fires at completion time (opposite of Claude).
required_changes: Obtain an observed model for Codex or explicitly mark model-identity unverifiable (fail-closed under Exact)
Normalize the ToolCall lifecycle phase to match the Claude adapter
Codex probe deferred (CLI not installed) — run when available
security_risks: no observed model -> silent model substitution undetectable for Codex
compatibility_risks: Exact policy cannot be honored for Codex without an observed-model source
required_acceptance_tests: Codex run under Exact halts if model identity cannot be proven

components.rationale: Excellent discipline: kill_on_drop -> dropped future SIGKILLs the child, concurrent stderr drain (4KiB tail), bounded 256-slot backpressure channel, timeout aborts+awaits so the child dies before return, hard bail on advisory&&write. Real-process tested. RECLASSIFIED adopt->adapt: reuse is NOT 'largely as-is' — it requires process-GROUP reaping (not just the direct child), a Sandboy boundary, pre-spawn environment isolation, and explicit async cancellation semantics (see required_changes).
required_changes: Reap the process GROUP (setsid/pgid), not just the direct child, so grandchildren (shells, MCP servers) are killed
Run inside Sandboy (kill_on_drop is process death, NOT a sandbox)
Async cancellation token instead of drop-only
security_risks: direct-child-only reap leaves grandchildren alive on cancel
no env scrub before spawn
compatibility_risks: none major; the primitive is transport-agnostic
required_acceptance_tests: cancel kills the whole process group
worker runs only inside a Sandboy boundary

components.rationale: Clean normalized worker-event contract, but per event-mapping.json it is lossy/incompatible/missing on the events 007 needs most: no permission.requested, no rate_limit, no model-info/drift, a single flat ToolCall (no start/complete, no id, inconsistent lifecycle across providers), and it is ephemeral (channel, never persisted).
required_changes: Add permission.requested, rate_limit, model-info/model-drift, tool.started/tool.completed(+id/status/output) variants
Persist events to the ledger with a monotonic seq (event.rs itself does not)
security_risks: missing model-drift event = the Exact kill-switch has no signal to fire on
compatibility_risks: ToolCall semantics differ per provider (request-time vs completion-time)
required_acceptance_tests: every 007-required event type round-trips through the ledger
tool start/complete are distinct events

components.rationale: FailureKind{ModelUnavailable,RateLimited,Transient} is a reasonable taxonomy but is derived by post-hoc lowercased substring matching of the error string, and conflates rate-limit with generic failure.
required_changes: Carry the classification (and retry-after/reset) as structured fields on the event, not string-matched after the fact
Separate a first-class RateLimited signal from Transient
security_risks: brittle string matching can misclassify (e.g. context-length as model-dead — Consilium guards this one case explicitly)
compatibility_risks: string patterns break when CLI wording changes
required_acceptance_tests: rate-limit classified from structured field, not text

components.rationale: OPERATOR DECISION (post Phase -1): council is REFERENCE ONLY (not vendored). It is well-shaped as a pure advisor (read-only, no verdict, ungrounded synthesis) and is a good design reference for a future advisory Skill, but it is not part of the spine and is not vendored now. Anonymization hides labels (random shuffle) but not content/self-authorship; scores are inert; synthesis is UNGROUNDED (no tests inside council).
required_changes: Package as a Skill whose output o7d treats as ungrounded opinion, never evidence
Optionally seed the shuffle for reproducibility
Shuffle review order to reduce residual positional bias
security_risks: majority/consensus could be mistaken for evidence if a consumer trusts it
compatibility_risks: none for source-of-truth: council never owned a verdict
required_acceptance_tests: o7d verdict is unchanged whether council agrees or not
council output is labeled advisory in the ledger

components.target_007_component: reference only (adopt the grounding-rule IDEA, not the engine)
components.rationale: OPERATOR DECISION (post Phase -1): conduct is REFERENCE ONLY. The grounding rule (failed tests force Accept->Rework) is the single most portable IDEA and directly serves DONE!=acceptance — 007 reimplements that idea with an o7d-owned verdict. The engine itself is not vendored: its decider is an LLM conductor (not a trusted plane), all workers share ONE in-place cwd sequentially (no worktree), disjoint-files is an unenforced prompt promise, failed writes are never rolled back, and no-verifier accepts on the agent's word.
required_changes: Relocate the accept/fail verdict to o7d (deterministic), keep conduct as a driver
Give each subtask/agent its own worktree (wire the dormant safety/git.rs)
Make no-verifier fail-closed
Roll back / discard failed-subtask writes
Separate Claude and Codex into separate runs+worktrees
security_risks: shared cwd = cross-worker clobber; LLM-owned verdict; prompt-injection surface in feedback interpolation
compatibility_risks: fundamentally divergent from 007's separate-worktree parallel model
required_acceptance_tests: two agents cannot see/clobber each other's files
a subtask with no verifier is never auto-accepted
verdict is computed by o7d, not the conductor model

components.rationale: Pure Kahn layering, fail-closed on dup-id/self-edge/unknown-dep/cycle, exhaustively unit-tested. Small and clean but trivial to reimplement in o7d's own types.
required_changes: reimplement against 007 RunGraph node types
compatibility_risks: wave concurrency is computed but unused by conduct (sequential)
required_acceptance_tests: cycle/unknown-dep rejected

components.rationale: Deterministic, timeout-bounded (600s SIGKILL), tamper-resistant build/test/lint execution; not-run is not a pass; lint advisory. Exactly the independent-gate signal a trusted control plane should compute. RECLASSIFIED adopt->adapt: reuse is NOT 'largely as-is' — it requires relocating the DECISION to o7d, a TRUSTED command source + wired trust store, a Sandboy boundary, and fail-CLOSED-on-absent-verifier (see required_changes).
required_changes: Move the DECISION to o7d (verify computes, o7d decides)
Source commands from a TRUSTED location + trust-on-first-use, never bare untrusted repo config
Run verify inside Sandboy (it executes sh -c)
Make absence-of-verifier fail-closed for acceptance
security_risks: executes arbitrary sh -c from possibly untrusted config = RCE if config is repo-sourced
output tails can leak secrets into the transcript
compatibility_risks: decision currently lives in conduct, must be lifted out
required_acceptance_tests: verify command from untrusted repo config is refused until operator-trusted
verify runs inside Sandboy
not-run verify blocks acceptance

components.rationale: Fail-closed on unparseable verdict (review_blocks=true); only Critical blocks; exposed via MCP with an explicit parse_ok contract. But lenient severity defaults unknown/missing/null to Minor (fail-OPEN for that field), and a transient reviewer failure degrades to Accept.
required_changes: Default unknown severity to the STRICTER end (or reject) instead of Minor
Make reviewer-unavailable fail-closed, not auto-accept
Keep review advisory to o7d, not a verdict
security_risks: fail-open severity + auto-accept-on-unavailable can wave through a bad diff
compatibility_risks: it is an LLM opinion, never a substitute for deterministic gates
required_acceptance_tests: malformed severity does not lower the block threshold
reviewer error does not auto-accept

components.rationale: This module IS automatic, always-on model switching over a ladder (demotes on unavailable/rate-limited/transient/even a spawn error), with no opt-out and no Exact mode. It is the direct antithesis of ModelPolicy::Exact and invariant 12. Its one virtue (loud stderr + recorded Fallback list) is worth referencing for an auditable, operator-consented fallback under a future ExplicitLadder policy.
required_changes: If any fallback is ever allowed, it must be a single-rung ladder by default and any demotion must HALT and require operator consent
security_risks: silent(ish) model substitution against an intended exact lock
compatibility_risks: incompatible with Exact by construction
required_acceptance_tests: under Exact, any model demotion halts the run

components.rationale: ModelHealth marks models dead/cold within a run and 'models --write' rewrites config to the newest catalog model. 'Keep-on-newest' is the opposite of an exact pin. (The catalog itself is a harmless reference list.)
required_changes: do not import auto-upgrade; keep a static, operator-pinned model registry
security_risks: auto model rewriting defeats exact-model provenance
compatibility_risks: conflicts with Exact
required_acceptance_tests: no code path rewrites the pinned model

components.target_007_component: event ledger storage shape + BudgetPolicy substrate
components.rationale: The only real DB: an append-only INSERT-only SQLite usage_log with a windowed aggregate and Mutex<Connection> for Arc sharing. The storage shape is exactly what an append-only ledger + BudgetPolicy wants. But it is pure accounting: no spend ceiling is enforced, and reads FAIL-OPEN to (0,0) on error (=most-free=preferred by routing).
required_changes: Extend schema from token rows to run-event rows + add crash-recovery reads
Add a real pre-spawn spend gate (BudgetPolicy)
Make reads fail-CLOSED
Treat estimated/unverified providers as worst-case, not equal to measured
Reconcile with Agent-SDK structured rate-limit events (retry-after/reset)
security_risks: fail-open quota read lets a corrupt DB read as unlimited budget
compatibility_risks: local tally is not provider-authoritative; cannot see other sessions' usage
required_acceptance_tests: corrupt/locked quota DB blocks (not permits) a run
budget ceiling stops a run before spawn

components.rationale: A post-hoc whole-file JSON snapshot written once at run END by the caller; a crash mid-run loses the entire transcript, and there is no reconnect/resume/cursor. This is the opposite of the append-only event ledger with crash/reconnect recovery 007 requires.
required_changes: build a per-event append + fsync ledger with a recovery reader (do not reuse this whole-file model)
security_risks: kind filename-safety is only a debug_assert
compatibility_risks: no cursor/resume — fundamentally divergent from invariant 9
required_acceptance_tests: a crash mid-run loses no committed events; resume replays from a cursor

components.rationale: Ladder model (RoleConfig.ladder()) is exactly where ModelPolicy::Exact vs ExplicitLadder would hook (a 1-rung ladder is legal). Fail-closed on malformed, fail-open on absent. BUT config is loaded from the cwd's consilium.config.json — i.e. an untrusted repo can supply model AND verify config.
required_changes: Add a ModelPolicy{Exact,ExplicitLadder} enum enforced in the failover choke point
Load trusted config from an o7d-owned location, NOT the target repo
security_risks: untrusted-repo config redirects models and verify commands
compatibility_risks: none structurally
required_acceptance_tests: model/verify config is never read from the untrusted target repo
Exact policy rejects a multi-rung ladder

components.rationale: Clean ProviderAuth taxonomy (Ready/NeedsLogin/CliMissing/Down) with actionable hints and concurrent live per-model liveness probes; never reads secret files (only references the OAuth env var). Note: 'preflight' here is model-reachability, NOT the (unwired) safety::preflight.
required_changes: Fold into o7d startup preflight; keep the exact-model probe honest (observed vs pinned)
security_risks: probes spawn CLIs (contain within Sandboy)
compatibility_risks: none
required_acceptance_tests: preflight fails closed if the pinned conductor model is unreachable

components.target_007_component: worktree-isolation crate (edit isolation, NOT a sandbox)
components.rationale: TOCTOU-hardened detached-worktree machinery: fd/device/inode-bound capability, owner-only 0700/0600, --no-checkout raw materialization (hooks/smudge/fsmonitor never run), fail-closed cleanup that preserves on any identity mismatch. Heavily test-proven (worktree_test.rs/trust_test.rs). BUT it is completely UNWIRED (no production caller) and it is edit-isolation, not process containment.
required_changes: Extract git.rs+fs.rs to a standalone crate (sever the ts-rs UI export on the inspection DTOs)
Add run-id association + RAII/typed cleanup contract (a dropped handle currently leaks)
Async wrappers + cancellation
o7d must be the SINGLE owner (git worktree prune --expire now is repo-global — two owners would race)
Pair with Sandboy; never call it a sandbox
security_risks: gives ZERO process containment — must be paired with Sandboy
cross-fs-device refusal + concurrent-creation are code-only (untested)
compatibility_risks: single-owner requirement; non-Unix fails closed
required_acceptance_tests: hostile repo hooks/filters/fsmonitor never execute
o7d is the sole worktree owner (no competing pruner)
a leaked handle is caught (RAII/typed contract)

components.rationale: TrustStore keys trust to (canonical repo, SHA-256 of exact command set); any command change revokes trust; owner-only, fd-bound, corrupt/symlinked state fails closed. commands.rs (provenance/digest) IS wired into verify.rs, but the TrustStore itself has NO production caller — so today a hostile repo's verify command runs with no trust check.
required_changes: WIRE the trust store into the verify path (trust-on-first-use before executing repo-sourced commands)
Present provenance (RepositoryConfig vs UserProvided) to the operator for consent
security_risks: currently absent enforcement = RCE-on-verify from untrusted repo config
compatibility_risks: none — the primitives are sound
required_acceptance_tests: a changed repo verify command re-prompts for trust
untrusted command is not executed without operator consent

components.target_007_component: reference only (007 builds its own typed delegation protocol)
components.rationale: OPERATOR DECISION (post Phase -1, see operator-decision.md): the temporary MCP bridge is REJECTED permanently — MCP bridge rejected, MCP runtime dependency none, Consilium MCP server not ported, schemas reference-only (no vendoring). A temporary bridge does not move toward the target enough to justify a second worker-launch path, a second delegation protocol, a separate cancellation model, no durable run identity, and a later mandatory removal. The six stateless stdio tools + the advisory/write-hardcoded, cwd-confined, structured-error patterns remain a useful REFERENCE for designing 007's own typed delegation protocol (delegation.requested via the ledger, o7d as sole source of truth).
required_changes: None — reference only. Build 007's own typed delegation: delegation.requested event -> o7d checks role/budget/depth/exact-model/permission/paths/gates -> o7d creates the child run + worktree.
security_risks: (if it were used) tool outputs flow to the conductor unfiltered; no authz beyond holding the stdio pipe — reasons it is not adopted
compatibility_risks: stdio single-client, no run identity — incompatible with o7d-as-source-of-truth, hence rejected
required_acceptance_tests: 007 delegation is a typed event bound to a run_id, not an MCP tool call

components.rationale: Router, REST snapshot handlers, origin_allowed (loopback/tauri-only, tested against suffix attacks), and cwd_within_root are reusable. But the run-lifecycle core ties run lifetime to the socket: cancel/close/EOF calls run.abort() -> kill_on_drop SIGKILLs the agent. That directly violates 'closing the browser must not stop the agent'.
required_changes: Rewrite handle_session: run lives in o7d, created by an authenticated command (UI never launches agents)
Socket becomes a cursor subscriber (?run_id&since=seq) over the ledger; socket close must NOT abort the run
Remove the semaphore-of-1; add multi-run/multi-conversation registry
security_risks: origin-only gate, no auth token (fine on loopback, not once network-exposed)
compatibility_risks: run-tied-to-socket is a hard divergence from invariants 2/8
required_acceptance_tests: closing the socket leaves the run alive and resumable
a new socket resumes from a cursor with no lost/dup events

components.rationale: Typed frames with snake_case tag discipline, a disjoint-tag guarantee test, and ts-rs single-source-of-truth codegen to the UI — an excellent typed-wire foundation.
required_changes: Add run_id, conversation_id, monotonic seq/cursor
Split subscribe-vs-create so a socket never starts a run
security_risks: SessionRequest.cwd is an untrusted-input deserialization boundary (already path-jailed)
compatibility_risks: frames currently assume one run per socket
required_acceptance_tests: every frame carries run_id + seq
a create and a subscribe are distinct operations

source_paths: ui/src (EventStream, EventRow, ResultPanel, SessionHeader, UsageBadge, QuotaDashboard, TableView, StatusPill)
components.rationale: The genuinely largely-as-is part: presentational components take plain props and never touch the socket; the reducer is a pure fold; parseFrame tolerantly forwards unknown tags. Wire-agnostic by construction.
required_changes: Minor reducer tweak: order/dedupe by seq for cursor replay (append-only-array assumption)
security_risks: none significant in the presentational layer
compatibility_risks: reducer assumes strictly-increasing arrival (fine after seq-dedupe)
required_acceptance_tests: reducer + components render unchanged against a new transport client

components.component: React UI — transport layer (useSession / protocol client / run creation)
components.rationale: This is the load-bearing rewrite the 'adopt UI' headline hides: useSession opens one socket per run, the UI itself sends the conduct frame (launches the agent directly), and there is no cursor/run_id. Reclassified out of the UI adopt so acquisition effort is not understated.
required_changes: Replace useSession with a cursor/ledger-subscribing client (?run_id&since=seq) dispatching the same SessionActions
Remove UI-initiated run creation — o7d owns run creation
Socket close must not stop the run
security_risks: UI-launches-agent-directly violates invariant 1 if carried over
compatibility_risks: one-socket-per-run model must become subscribe-to-run
required_acceptance_tests: reload restores workspace from the ledger cursor with no lost/dup events
UI cannot start a run directly

components.rationale: Embeds the server in-process and binds server lifetime to the app process — the exact anti-pattern for an agent that must survive backgrounding/kill on android-first. The Android asset scaffolding exists but the architecture does not translate.
required_changes: Do not reuse the in-process lifecycle; if a native shell is kept, it is a thin authenticated VIEWER over a standalone/remote o7d
Salvage only the icon/asset scaffolding and the runtime.ts backend-resolution seam
security_risks: in-process localhost server lifetime coupled to a killable app
compatibility_risks: directly violates invariants 1/2/8 for android-first
required_acceptance_tests: agent survives the shell/app being killed
